trans-unit.source: BCH Explorer is a blockchain and mempool explorer for the Bitcoin Cash community, created by Melroy van den Berg. It is a completely self-hosted without any trusted third-parties.
If you like this project and use the explorer on a regular basis, please consider donating.
Maintaining, improving and creating this BCH explorer costs a lot of time and effort. As a one-man operation, I spend a lot of personal time working on this project.

By sponsoring me you are supporting my work and ensuring that the BCH Explorer project remains up-to-date, stable and available to the whole Bitcoin Cash community. As well as extending this BCH Explorer for specific BCH features. Are you interested in becoming an enterprise sponsor and getting your company listed on this site? Feel free to reach out via Telegram.
Learn more about the BCH Explorer open-source project. Discover enterprise sponsors, integrations, FOSS licensing, and more.
See mempool transactions, confirmed transactions, balance, and more for Bitcoin Cash address .

There are too many transactions on this address, more than your backend can handle. See more on setting up a stronger backend. Consider viewing this address on the official BCH Explorer website instead: https://bchexplorer.cash/address/
View Bitcoin Cash ASERT difficulty adjustment deviation over time.
See Bitcoin Cash feerates visualized over time, including minimum and maximum feerates per block along with feerates at various percentiles.
See the average mining fees earned per Bitcoin Cash block visualized in BCH and USD over time.
See the mining fees earned per Bitcoin Cash block compared to the Bitcoin Cash block subsidy, visualized in BCH and USD over time.
select filter categories to highlight matching transactions

See Bitcoin Cash block health visualized over time. Block health is a measure of how many expected transactions were included in an actual mined block. Expected transactions are determined using BCH Explorer's re-implementation of BCHN's transaction selection algorithm.
No data to display yet. Try again later.
Your browser does not support this feature.
See Bitcoin Cash block rewards in BCH and USD visualized over time. Block rewards are the total funds miners earn from the block subsidy and fees.
See Bitcoin Cash block sizes (MB) visualized over time.
See Bitcoin Cash block time variation over time.
See the average number of transactions per Bitcoin Cash block over time.
See size, fee range, included transactions, audit (expected vs actual), and more for Bitcoin Cash block ().
See the total UTXO throughput and BCH transaction volume per Bitcoin Cash block over time.
See size, weight, fee range, included transactions, audit (expected v actual), and more for Bitcoin block ().
This block does not belong to the main chain, it has been replaced by:
Based on average native transaction of 140 Bytes

trans-unit.note: Total subsidy and fees in a block

trans-unit.source: There is a stale block at this height
There are stale blocks at this height
See the most recent Bitcoin Cash blocks along with basic stats such as block height, block reward, block size, and more.
Either 2x the minimum, or the Low Priority rate (whichever is lower)
Usually places your transaction in between the second and third mempool blocks
Usually places your transaction in between the first and second mempool blocks
Places your transaction in the first mempool block
See hashrate and difficulty for the Bitcoin network visualized over time.
See Bitcoin Cash mining pool dominance visualized over time: see how top mining pools' share of total hashrate has fluctuated over time.
See stats for Bitcoin Cash transactions in the mempool: fee range, aggregate size, and more. Mempool blocks are updated in real-time as the network receives new transactions.
Get real-time Bitcoin Cash mining stats like hashrate, difficulty adjustment, block rewards, pool dominance, and more.
The overall luck of all mining pools over the past week. A luck bigger than 100% means the average block time for the current epoch is less than 10 minutes.
How many unique pools found at least one block over the past week.
The number of blocks found over the past week.
See the top Bitcoin Cash mining pools ranked by number of blocks mined, over your desired timeframe.
See mining pool stats for : most recent mined blocks, hashrate over time, total block reward to date, known coinbase addresses, and more.
Overview of the mining pools on Bitcoin Cash.
See the Bitcoin Cash price in USD visualized over time.
Broadcast a transaction to the Bitcoin Cash network using the transaction's hash.

trans-unit.note: Tooltip for the pause/resume updates button
Accessible label for the pause/resume updates button

trans-unit.source: See the most recent transactions on the Bitcoin Cash network, updated in real-time.
Amount being paid to miners in the past 144 blocks
Average fees per block in the past 144 blocks
Fee paid on average for each transaction in the past 144 blocks
Explore the full Bitcoin Cash ecosystem
Search by student name or ID...
Significant Bitcoin Cash blocks, including halvings, hard forks, protocol upgrades, and other milestones — past and future.
Significant Bitcoin Cash blocks, including halvings, hard forks, protocol upgrades, and other milestones — past and future.
This node hasn't seen any stale blocks yet!
See the most recent stale chain tips on the Bitcoin network.
See mempool size (in MB) and transactions per second (in B/s) visualized over time.
This transaction was projected to be included in the block
This transaction was seen in the mempool prior to mining
This transaction was missing from our mempool prior to mining
This transaction may have been added out-of-band
This transaction conflicted with another version in our mempool

trans-unit.note: Transaction ETA not any time soon

trans-unit.source: Get real-time status, addresses, fees, script info, and more for Bitcoin Cash transaction with txid .
Transaction hex or base64 encoded PSBT
This transaction is stored locally in your browser. Broadcast it to add it to the mempool.
trans-unit.note: This transaction is stored locally in your browser.

trans-unit.source: Transaction cannot be broadcasted because it's missing or has an invalid signature
Preview a transaction to the Bitcoin network using the transaction's raw hex data.
This transaction ID appears twice in the blockchain as the coinbase of blocks and , due to a bug later mitigated by BIP-30.
Waiting for it to appear in the mempool...
Warning! This transaction involves deceptively similar addresses. It may be an address poisoning attack.
Show more inputs to reveal fee data
This transaction has a low transaction fee on L1, because of BCH
Only ~ sat/B was needed to get into this block
See the total number of unspent transaction outputs (UTXOs) on the Bitcoin Cash network over time.
Provide a fallback fee if the finalized transaction size can't be estimated reliably (hidden scripts in P2SH).
Double check that this transaction sends the funds back to the provided address before signing
See mempool transactions, confirmed transactions, balance, and more for Bitcoin Cash wallet .

bchexplorer.cash merely provides data about the Bitcoin Cash network. It cannot help you with retrieving funds, wallet issues, etc. For any such requests, you need to get in touch with the entity that helped make the transaction (wallet software, exchange company, etc).
Get answers to common questions like: What is a mempool? What is a blockchain? How can I run my own instance of BCH Explorer? And more.
Documentation for the bchexplorer.cash REST API service: get info on addresses, transactions, blocks, fees, mining, and more.
Documentation for the bchexplorer.cash WebSocket API service: get real-time info on blocks, mempools, transactions, addresses, and more.
Documentation for our Electrum RPC interface: get instant, convenient, and reliable access to a Fulcrum instance.
This address is deceptively similar to another output. It may be part of an address poisoning attack.
How to host my own instance?
Your balance is too low.Please top up your account.
This is a test network. Coins have no value.
